Petition For Orders To: 1) Compel Trustee To Provide Trust Information And An Accounting In Compliance With The Probate Code; 2) Determine Breach Of
Off Calendar Apr-29-2026 Continued To Jul-22-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Cameren Ripoli appeared via zoom for Katheryn Flowers and Stephanie Arnold (petitioners).
Kevin Jackson appeared via zoom for Nelda Arnold (respondent).

The Court acknowledged the settlement agreement, and that once it's filed the Court shall set a new court date.

The Amended Petition for Orders Etc., is continued to July 22, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Status on Mediation is ordered off calendar.



Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Petition For Orders To: 1) Compel Trustee To Provide Trust Information And An Accounting In Compliance With The Probate Code; 2) Determine Breach Of
Off Calendar Dec-02-2025 Continued To Apr-29-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Cameren Ripoli, for and with Stephanie Henry, and Kathryn Flowers (petitioners).
Kevin Jackson, for Nelda Arnos (respondent).

At the outset, Mr. Jackson offers an update regarding mediation of this matter reporting that the parties are scheduled to mediate on Apr-06-2026. After discussion regarding how counsel wishes to proceed, counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the next hearing date for the subject matters. After additional discussion, including whether Mr. Jackson is to file objections, the subject matters are continued to Apr-29-2026 at 9:00 AM in Dept. 204. Any objections are to be filed prior to the next calling. A Status of Mediation shall be added to calendar on Apr-15-2026 at 9:00 AM in Dept. 204 as confirmed in open court.

Judge: Hon. Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Ramirez. Not Reported.

Off Calendar Jul-23-2025 Continued To Oct-01-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Cameren Ripoli appeared via zoom for Kathryn Flowers and Stephanie Henry (petitioners).
Kevin Jackson appeared via for Nelda Arnos (respondent).

Mr. Jackson stated that parties met after mediation, that status report was filed, and that he will draft the initial settlement agreement.

The matters are continued to October 1, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Objections to be filed by September 17, 2025.


Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Ng. Not reported.

Petition For Orders To: 1) Compel Trustee To Provide Trust Information And An Accounting In Compliance With The Probate Code; 2) Determine Breach Of
Off Calendar Feb-19-2025 Continued To Jul-23-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together:
PTR-24-307874, In re: The Qualified Marital Deduction Trust and the Exemption Trust Established Under the Henry/Arnold Revocable Trust;
PTR-24-307895, In re: The Exemption Trust Established Under the Henry/Arnold Revocable Trust;
PTR-24-307896, In re: The Qualified Marital Deduction Trust Established Under the W. Dean Henry Legacy Partners Business Assets Revocable Trust;
PTR-24-307897, In re: The Exemption Trust Established Under the W. Dean Henry Legacy Partners Business Assets Revocable Trust

Appearances:
Ashley Day for Kathryn Flowers and Stephanie Henry, Petitioners (via Zoom).
Kevin Jackson for Nelda Arnold, Respondent (via Zoom).
Stephanie Henry is present via Zoom.

Discussion was held regarding a recently filed status report. The parties confirmed agreement with the statements in the status report. Ms. Day reported the agreed upon terms as follows: the trustee's accounting would be provided by April 30, 2025. The parties agreed to mediate by June 30, 2025 and the trust would cover mediation costs. If mediation is unsuccessful, the trustee's objections will be due three weeks after the mediation date.

The court inquired whether the trustee was making a general appearance in each case without objecting to personal jurisdiction in California. Mr. Jackson confirmed that the trustee did not object to jurisdiction.

Mr. Jackson proposed consolidating the four separate cases, stating that the trusts were nearly identical and became irrevocable at death. Ms. Day agreed and noted that the matters had originally been filed as one, but the clerk's office had required them to be split. The court directed counsel to submit a proposed order of consolidation and serve it on all parties.

The court agreed to issue a Breslin order covering all four cases. The court continued all four matters to July 23, 2025, to allow time for mediation.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
